description:
The sudo <tt>passwd_timeout</tt> tag sets the amount of time sudo password prompt waits.
On Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9, the default <tt>passwd_timeout</tt> value is 5 minutes.

The passwd_timeout should be configured by making sure that the
<tt>passwd_timeout=<sub idref="var_sudo_passwd_timeout" /></tt> tag exists in
<tt>/etc/sudoers</tt> configuration file or any sudo configuration snippets
in <tt>/etc/sudoers.d/</tt>.
rationale: Reducing the time <tt>sudo</tt> waits for a a password reduces the time the process is exposed.
ocil_clause: passwd_timeout is not set with the appropriate value for sudo
ocil:
To determine if passwd_timeout has been configured for sudo, run the following command: 
$ sudo grep -ri '^Defaults.*passwd_timeout=' /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.d/
The command should return a matching output.
title: Ensure sudo passwd_timeout is appropriate - sudo passwd_timeout
